deep_dive.title: On Optimal Data Splitting for Split Conformal Prediction
deep_dive.abstract: Conformal prediction and its variants, including the split conformal prediction, provide a distribution-free framework for uncertainty quantification by constructing prediction intervals or sets with finite-sample coverage guarantees. The statistical efficiency of these intervals depends critically on how the data are split into training and calibration samples. Despite its practical importance, a principled characterization of the training-calibration split that minimizes prediction interval length while maintaining coverage has remained largely unresolved. In this paper, we develop a theoretical framework for optimal data splitting in split conformal prediction. We first analyze the problem in a general setting and derive analytical characterizations of the length-optimal split ratio under both symmetric and asymmetric regimes. We then show how the general results specialize to several commonly used regression settings, including linear regression, nonparametric regression, and neural networks, thereby demonstrating the scope of the framework. We also describe a data-based method for selecting the optimal proportion. Our analysis clarifies how model-related features govern the optimal allocation of samples between training and calibration and provides principled guidance for constructing shorter prediction intervals. Experiments on both synthetic and real-world datasets demonstrate the applicability of the proposed methodology across a variety of practical scenarios.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: optimal data splitting for split conformal prediction
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 分割共形预测的最优数据划分
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 分割共形预测的最优数据划分
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 分割共形预测的最优数据划分
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Develops theoretical framework for optimal training-calibration split in split conformal prediction to minimize interval length while maintaining coverage.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 分割共形预测中训练集与校准集的划分比例直接影响预测区间长度，但缺乏理论指导。该论文建立了最优数据划分的理论框架，在一般设置下推导了区间长度的解析表达式，并给出最小化长度同时保持覆盖的划分准则。通过理论分析和实验验证，为实践者提供了可操作的数据划分原则。

deep_dive.title: Relational and Sequential Conformal Inference for Energy Time Series over Graphs via Foundation Models
deep_dive.abstract: Accurate energy demand forecasting is essential for the reliable operation and planning of modern sustainable energy systems. Spatial-temporal graph neural networks (STGNNs) have recently achieved strong performance in point forecasting by jointly modeling temporal dynamics and relational dependencies across interconnected energy nodes. However, in real-world energy systems, accurate point forecasts alone are insufficient, as operators also require reliable uncertainty estimates to support risk-aware decision-making, grid stability, and operational planning under uncertainty. Conformal prediction provides a principled and model-agnostic framework for uncertainty quantification with statistical coverage guarantees, making it particularly attractive for safety-critical energy applications. However, existing conformal prediction approaches often fail to fully capture the complex spatial-temporal structure of energy systems. To address these limitations, we propose STOIC (Spatial-Temporal Graph Conformal Prediction with In-Context Learning), a novel framework that integrates graph-based forecasting with the zero-shot calibration capabilities of tabular foundation models. STOIC first generates point forecasts using an STGNN and subsequently reformulates spatial-temporal residuals into a tabular representation suitable for in-context learning. Leveraging a tabular foundation model, STOIC calibrates prediction intervals without task-specific retraining, effectively capturing both sequential and relational dependencies. We evaluate STOIC on five diverse benchmarks, including synthetic simulations as well as real-world electricity and district heating networks. Across all datasets, STOIC consistently outperforms existing conformal prediction baselines, delivering more reliable and robust uncertainty estimates for complex graph-structured energy time series.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes conformal inference for energy time series to provide uncertainty quantification with coverage guarantees
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出能源时间序列的共形推断，提供具有覆盖保证的不确定性量化
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出能源时间序列的共形推断，提供具有覆盖保证的不确定性量化
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出能源时间序列的共形推断，提供具有覆盖保证的不确定性量化
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Extends conformal prediction to relational and sequential energy time series over graphs, leveraging foundation models for distribution-free uncertainty quantification.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对能源时间序列预测中缺乏可靠不确定性估计的问题，提出关系与序列共形推断方法。该方法基于共形预测框架，利用图结构上的基础模型，对时空图神经网络的点预测进行分布自由的不确定性量化，并具有统计覆盖保证。在真实能源数据集上的实验表明，该方法能够提供有效的预测区间，支持风险感知决策。

deep_dive.title: Approximate full-conformal multi-task regression with reproducing kernels
deep_dive.abstract: Multi-task regression aims at jointly solving multiple regression problems, called tasks. Compared to solving each task separately, better performances can be achieved as long as the tasks are sufficiently related. Full-conformal prediction is a framework that formulates a data-dependent prediction-region containing the unknown output-vector at any prescribed confidence level. However, explicit computation of this prediction-region is intractable in general since it requires training infinitely many predictors. The present work focuses on multi-task regression in a Reproducing Kernel Hilbert Space (RKHS) of vector-valued functions. This computational issue is addressed by designing an approximating predictionregion containing the full-conformal one. This construction is carried out in two scenarios: piq when the inter-task covariance-matrix is known, and piiq when this matrix is estimated. In terms of volume, the tightness of this approximation is assessed theoretically by means of an upper-bound in the first scenario. It is also empirically proved to improve upon the split-conformal prediction on synthetic data in both scenarios.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 全共形预测用于多任务回归
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 全共形预测用于多任务回归
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 全共形预测用于多任务回归
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes an approximate full-conformal prediction region for multi-task regression in RKHS, addressing computational intractability.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对多任务回归中的全共形预测计算困难问题，在再生核希尔伯特空间框架下设计了包含全共形预测区域的近似预测区域。该方法保证了任意置信水平下的分布自由不确定性量化，从而在多任务联合回归中实现了理论上可靠的预测集。实验表明近似区域紧密逼近精确区域，为共形预测在多输出场景中的实际应用提供了可行方案。

deep_dive.title: Predicting Heterogeneous Treatment Effects Of Building Energy Saving Retrofits Using Causal Machine Learning
deep_dive.abstract: Information Systems research increasingly relies on machine learning (ML) to predict outcomes in complex sociotechnical systems, yet predictive models are not designed to identify causal effects. This limitation is particularly critical in building retrofits, where unbiased estimates of energy savings are essential for climate policy and investment decisions. Because retrofit adoption is shaped by household and building characteristics that also affect energy consumption, predictive ML can yield biased effect estimates. This paper systematically benchmarks leading causal ML estimators, including metalearners (S-, T- and X-Learners) and DoubleML across multiple retrofit interventions. To enable this comparison, we construct a physically grounded simulation in which true treatment effects and realistic adoption biases are known. Results show that DoubleML achieves the lowest estimation errors, particularly for complex envelope retrofits. These findings demonstrate that orthogonalising the treatment assignment improves causal effect estimation and provides a methodological foundation for large-scale energy retrofit and policy evaluation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: benchmarks causal ML estimators for heterogeneous treatment effects in building retrofits
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 对建筑改造中的异质性处理效应进行因果机器学习基准测试
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 对建筑改造中的异质性处理效应进行因果机器学习基准测试
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 对建筑改造中的异质性处理效应进行因果机器学习基准测试
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Benchmarks causal ML methods (metalearners, DoubleML) for estimating heterogeneous treatment effects of building energy retrofits. Shows biased estimates from predictive ML and demonstrates necessity of causal ML for unbiased policy guidance.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 信息系统研究中使用机器学习预测结果，但预测模型无法识别因果效应，这在建筑节能改造中尤为关键，因为装修采纳的非随机性会导致偏差。本文系统对比了元学习器和双重机器学习等因果推断方法，在多个改造干预上进行基准测试。结果显示因果机器学习能够提供无偏的异质性处理效应估计，避免误判。这为建筑能效政策制定提供了稳健可靠的工具，同时也展示了因果机器学习在复杂社会技术系统中的适用性。

quick_skim.title: DRIFTLENS: Measuring Memory-Induced Reasoning Drift in Personalized Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Personalization changes what a model says to a user; we show that it can also change the reasoning trajectory used to justify the response. Modern LLMs personalize interactions by storing user attributes, preferences, and prior context, then injecting this information into future prompts. We study whether such memory reshapes reasoning on open-ended questions where no single ground-truth answer exists. To quantify this effect, we introduce DRIFTLENS, a ground-truth-free framework that maps each expressed reasoning step to a value category and measures divergence between a question's no-memory trajectory and its trajectory under injected user-attribute memory. We first validate that DRIFTLENS distinguishes content-free pragmatic noise from substantive reasoning changes. Across four LLMs and 10 user-attribute categories, including age, occupation, and disability, user-attribute memory induces medium-to-large reasoning drift above each model's pragmatic-noise floor, even when final answers remain fluent, on-topic, and plausible. We then evaluate GRPO- and DPO-based post-training methods for reducing drift. Both reduce drift, but neither uniformly dominates; effects on downstream capability, helpfulness, and instruction following are model-and reward-dependent. These results suggest that memory-induced reasoning drift is a measurable and only partly mitigated failure mode of personalized language models.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Counterfactual evaluation of memory effects on LLM reasoning trajectories
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 对LLM推理轨迹中记忆影响的反事实评估
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 对LLM推理轨迹中记忆影响的反事实评估
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 对LLM推理轨迹中记忆影响的反事实评估
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: DRIFTLENS measures reasoning drift in personalized LLMs by comparing no-memory and with-memory trajectories, enabling counterfactual evaluation of memory effects.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文研究了个性化记忆如何改变大语言模型的推理轨迹，提出了DRIFTLENS框架，通过对比无记忆和注入记忆后的推理路径，量化推理漂移。该框架无需真实标签即可评估记忆对推理的影响，为反事实评估大模型输出提供了新工具，有助于理解个性化带来的推理变化。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文研究了个性化记忆如何改变大语言模型的推理轨迹，提出了DRIFTLENS框架，通过对比无记忆和注入记忆后的推理路径，量化推理漂移。该框架无需真实标签即可评估记忆对推理的影响，为反事实评估大模型输出提供了新工具，有助于理解个性化带来的推理变化。

quick_skim.title: Mobility Safe Adaptive Reserve Certification for Electric Vehicle Hydrogen Bus and Building Resilience Hubs
quick_skim.abstract: Zero-emission mobility depots are becoming resilience assets because one site can host EV charging, hydrogen-bus operation, stationary conversion equipment, and nearby critical-building backup. The key question is not raw outage export capacity: hydrogen exported to buildings can strand buses, EV availability is stochastic, and building demand shifts seasonally. We introduce a mobility-safe reserve certification framework for a coupled EV, hydrogen-bus, and critical-building hub. It combines a physics-hybrid universal differential equation building-load twin, one-sided split conformal reserve calibration, adaptive conformal inference for seasonal drift, and a mobility-first scheduling rule that protects post-event bus service before assigning hydrogen to buildings. Evaluation uses 495,221 real EV charging sessions across eight regions, AC Transit GTFS-derived hydrogen-bus service days, and EnergyPlus 25.2 simulations under real TMY3 weather. Across 66,816 held-out outage scenarios, a mobility-blind hydrogen-export policy served 39.2\% of building demand but protected buses in 0\% of cases and caused a 426.7 kg mean bus-hydrogen shortfall. A nominal mean-resource promise delivered only 45.4\% of commitments. The certified mobility-first policy was the only tested policy to achieve 100\% commitment delivery, 100\% bus protection, and zero mean bus-hydrogen shortfall, while serving 20.5\% of critical-building demand. Under a summer-to-winter load shift, adaptive conformal inference raised late-period empirical coverage from 0.687 to 0.831 and reached 0.891 overall coverage against a 0.90 target with lower mean reserve than static split conformal. Across 12 building/seed drift runs, it kept low late-coverage variability and the lowest mean reserve. These results show that resilience value in shared zero-emission hubs depends on service-aware certification, not raw export capacity alone.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Split conformal reserve calibration and adaptive conformal inference for energy hub resilience
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 在能源枢纽韧性中应用分裂共形保留校准和自适应共形推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 在能源枢纽韧性中应用分裂共形保留校准和自适应共形推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 在能源枢纽韧性中应用分裂共形保留校准和自适应共形推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Applies split conformal calibration and adaptive conformal inference to certify mobility-safe reserve capacity in coupled EV, hydrogen, and building hubs, handling stochasticity and drift.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对零排放交通枢纽的韧性认证问题，提出了一种结合物理混合模型、单侧分裂共形保留校准和自适应共形推理的框架。框架在电动汽车、氢能巴士和关键建筑相互耦合的场景下，处理EV可用性的随机性和季节漂移，并采用移动优先调度规则保护灾后出行能力。实验表明，该方法能在分布自由的假设下提供可靠的容量认证，保障关键基础设施的韧性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对零排放交通枢纽的韧性认证问题，提出了一种结合物理混合模型、单侧分裂共形保留校准和自适应共形推理的框架。框架在电动汽车、氢能巴士和关键建筑相互耦合的场景下，处理EV可用性的随机性和季节漂移，并采用移动优先调度规则保护灾后出行能力。实验表明，该方法能在分布自由的假设下提供可靠的容量认证，保障关键基础设施的韧性。

quick_skim.title: Inference for Group Interaction Experiments
quick_skim.abstract: A common experimental research design is one in which individuals are randomly allocated into groups that then interact under different group-level treatment conditions. We develop design-based inference for such "group interaction" experiments, covering scenarios in which groups are either fixed or randomly formed and in which potential outcomes are either fixed relative to others' group assignments or subject to interference. For each scenario, we characterize the causal estimand that the design targets and the inferential strategy appropriate to it. Working in a sparse-sampling asymptotic regime, we show that cluster-robust inference remains consistent and accounts for dependencies from various sources when interference is present, delivering valid inference on marginalized exposure effects. When interference is absent and groups are formed randomly, the design reduces to an individually randomized experiment, and individual-level heteroskedasticity-robust inference suffices for the average treatment effect. Our results on the asymptotic distribution of commonly used estimators rely on a novel coupling strategy that may be useful for design-based inference in other complex experiments.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: design-based inference for group interaction experiments
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 群体交互实验的设计推断
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 群体交互实验的设计推断
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 群体交互实验的设计推断
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Develops design-based causal inference for group interaction experiments, covering interference and cluster-robust inference.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文为群体交互实验开发设计推断方法，涵盖固定或随机形成的群体，以及在干扰存在下的因果估计。在稀疏抽样渐近下，证明聚类稳健推断一致且能解释多种依赖来源，为这类实验提供了有效的推断策略。
